mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenue expectations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive movement of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, causing equity valuations to contract and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in greater than 60% of observations when CPIAUCSL remains above 230.0, or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply chain efficiency, which increases corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day SPX returns across all market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment increase exceeding 150,000 signals robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which drives consumer spending and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases greater than 150.0 in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in greater than 50% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 150,000, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures compress corporate margins and trigger equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of declining GDPC1 and rising CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of ultra-low interest rates where FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0%, investors shift capital from fixed income to equities in search of yield, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no upward bias when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or the signal consistently underperforms a buy-and-hold baseline during low-rate regimes.
